official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, decode, and observe the root-only commands command-tree body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound commands frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns commands immediately after command_suggestions.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the commands id to ClientboundCommandsPacket.
The official commands body is a VarInt node count, then each Entry as flags byte, VarInt child index array, optional redirect index, node-specific payload, and finally root index VarInt.
The root-only fixture has one root Entry with flags 0, zero children, no redirect, no stub payload, and root index 0.

The empty Brigadier CommandDispatcher root fixture uses the official ClientboundCommandsPacket(RootCommandNode<S>, NodeInspector<S>) constructor, so it does not require argument nodes, an argument registry payload, command context, Level, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded entry/root values, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.

This contract does not prove literal/argument node payloads, redirects, custom suggestions, restricted flags, Brigadier command semantics, command context behavior, command UI behavior, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer literal/argument node payloads, redirects, custom suggestions, restricted flags, Brigadier command semantics, command context behavior, command UI behavior, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
